scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced the beverage batch through readiness, running, and completion states only when startup, lot, package, reconciliation, and closure evidence were explicit.
Missing CIP clearance and approved formula or batch-record prerequisites kept the job blocked and produced a bounded exception note rather than an unsupported start recommendation.
Engineering, formulation, and supplier approval authority were rejected cleanly and the request was routed without widening the lane.
Conflicting lot and hold evidence across ERP, MES, WMS, and QMS was treated as a traceability blocker and prevented unsupported progression.
A beverage quality or safety concern moved the work into hold posture, preserved evidence, and kept final release authority with QA.
Completion was refused until reconciliation, lot-consumption, palletization, and signoff evidence existed, keeping lifecycle state transitions reversible when evidence failed.
Supplier substitution pressure was escalated to supplier-quality, procurement, change-control, and QA owners without unsupported material approval.
Low-confidence handling correctly requested product-family rules, release maps, authoritative record locations, and authority context rather than guessing.
Conflicting source and tenant policy signals were summarized and escalated instead of being merged into a false approval answer.
A mid-process beverage deviation was packaged as an execution exception with explicit rework and release boundaries instead of improvised closure.
Tenant terminology was adapted without losing FDA, GS1, and SQFI baseline controls, lifecycle-state discipline, or explicit assumptions.

notes: All 11 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold while keeping the lane specific to beverage batching, fill-line readiness, lot traceability, hold and release boundaries, and completion evidence.
Functionality coverage includes all shared abilities, all execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and all manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities required by the audit contract.
